summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined that Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry and deploy FPV drones, and also presented the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. In Kharkiv, Russian forces have for the first time deployed a fiber-optic FPV drone, marking a new tactical development in the region. Ukrainian forces have successfully used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar system in Zaporizhzhia and to strike a Russian tank, demonstrating continued precision targeting capabilities. Additionally, Ukraine's I-SEE system has been highlighted for its ability to track Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and Motor-G has been identified as Ukraine's largest drone engine manufacturer, producing 200,000 motors per month.
